head: MetalNutObject
evidence: Metal nut scratch is selected as a development RCA case because linear surface marks are visually consistent with contact or handling damage

head: ScratchDefect
evidence: Scratch is a linear surface defect consistent with contact damage

head: MechanicalContact
evidence: Mechanical contact is grouped under handling-related damage

head: ScratchDefect
evidence: Repeated surface abrasion can produce shallow scratch-like marks

head: ColorDefect
evidence: Localized color change can indicate residue staining or foreign material on the surface

head: ColorDefect
evidence: Color variation can also reflect material or coating inconsistency

head: CableObject
evidence: Cable cut defects are selected as development RCA cases for mechanical or assembly-related damage

head: CutDefect
evidence: Cut-like cable damage is visually consistent with sharp contact or cutting damage

head: MissingWireDefect
evidence: Missing wire evidence maps to a missing component root-cause category

head: MissingWireDefect
evidence: Missing wire evidence can be explained by an assembly omission or placement error

head: BentWireDefect
evidence: Bent wire evidence can indicate assembly or routing error

head: BentWireDefect
evidence: Bent wire evidence can also indicate fixture or positioning misalignment

head: CrackDefect
evidence: Cracks can indicate brittle material or formation defects

head: CrackDefect
evidence: Cracks can also be consistent with compression during packaging or transport

head: ContaminationDefect
evidence: Visible contamination maps to a foreign material or residue source

head: SqueezeDefect
evidence: Squeeze-like deformation is consistent with compression or packaging pressure

head: SqueezeDefect
evidence: Squeeze-like deformation can also be caused by handling damage
